requirement: Buyer can create a sourcing event (RFI, RFQ, RFP) with customizable templates.
Buyer can manage and track sourcing events in real-time with automated workflows and notifications.
Buyer can collaborate with suppliers in real-time through the platform.
Buyer can evaluate suppliers based on their bids and proposals.
Buyer can award contracts to selected suppliers.
Buyer can manage and track document revisions and approvals.
Buyer can initiate a sourcing request form with required information.
Supplier can participate and respond to sourcing events via email.
Evaluators can manually collate and evaluate all responses in a spreadsheet.
Procurement buyer can review and approve the comparative schedule.
New suppliers can be onboarded by procurement buyer.
LOA can approve awards.
Procurement buyer can award suppliers.
Advanced reporting capabilities provide actionable insights into sourcing events, supplier performance, and spend data.
Coupa supports automated scoring and evaluation of supplier responses.
Requester can submit a sourcing request form for approval by the line manager.
Procurement Owner can create a sourcing event in draft status after approval of the sourcing request.
Procurement Buyer can initiate a sourcing event in one of three ways: from scratch, from an existing template, or by copying from a previously conducted event.
Procurement Buyer can configure event settings, including event name, currency, and basic information.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers, set evaluations, and configure event timelines.
Procurement Buyer can define the type of sourcing event being created, including RFx stage, allow multiple responses, and hide supplier responses.
Procurement Buyer can attach terms and conditions or other relevant documents for suppliers to accept.
Procurement Buyer can create an event and invite internal team members to conduct specific tasks.
Procurement Buyer can configure event details such as attachments, forms, and event timeline.
Procurement Buyer can add items individually or in bulk, including uploading data from CSV, and configure item details such as quantity, need by date, and total cost formula.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event, including selecting from the Coupa supplier list, copying from a previous event, or loading from CSV.
Procurement Buyer can manually add evaluators for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with evaluation criteria, weights, and evaluators.
Procurement Buyer can launch the sourcing event and make it visible to suppliers.
Supplier receives an invitation email with a link to the login page and system-generated OTP for accessing the event.
Supplier must accept Terms and Conditions before participating in the event.
Supplier can access and participate in the event after accepting Terms and Conditions.
Procurement Buyer and Supplier can communicate through a live chat window.
Procurement Buyer can edit the event, including weights for scoring and planned savings, after launching it.
Procurement Buyer can invite suppliers to an event.
Supplier can submit responses to the event.
Procurement Buyer can view event details, including invited suppliers and responses.
Procurement Buyer can send email reminders to suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can extend the timeline of the event.
Event can be auto-closed or manually ended by Procurement Buyer.
Procurement Buyer can view and evaluate supplier responses.
Procurement Buyer can request internal users for evaluation.
Procurement Buyer can create a follow-on event for further negotiations.
Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Evaluator can add scores and evaluations for supplier responses.
Procurement Team can review evaluator's evaluations.
Sourcing owner can decide on the event award based on evaluations.
Procurement Buyer can award the selected winner(s) in the Sourcing event.
Once approvals are completed, the event is marked as awarded.
Procurement Buyer can flip the Sourcing event into a new Requisition.
Requester can create a purchase requisition and submit it for approval.
Procurement Buyer can initiate the sourcing process by flipping a purchase requisition to a sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event from scratch, using a template, or copying from a previous event.
Procurement Buyer can configure sourcing event settings, timelines, details, add suppliers, and set evaluations.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with various options such as event type, terms and conditions, and event team.
Procurement Buyer can configure event details such as attachments, forms, and items.
Procurement Buyer can define timelines specific to the event, including event start and end dates, and invite internal team members to conduct tasks.
Suppliers can bid in different currencies, and their responses will be evaluated in the event currency.
Suppliers can add attachments while communicating on the message centre.
Procurement Buyer can track the sourcing event, including the ability to convert the event into 1 Envelope or 2 Envelope by selecting the Unseal manually option.
Cost avoidance and planned savings can be tracked and reported.
Buyer can add and invite suppliers to sourcing events.
Buyer can add evaluators and assign weights to specific categories for sourcing events.
Buyer can launch and publish sourcing events, and edit event details after launch.
Buyer can add items to purchase orders, including searching for preloaded options and manually entering prices.
Buyer can configure event settings, including terms and conditions, event type, and currency.
